rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition Of Sarah Smith To Perpetuate Testimony Of Anthony Adams By Videotaped Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, July 6, 2023 in Department 503, Line 1. The Petition of Sarah Smith to Perpetuate Testimony of Anthony Adams by Videotaped Deposition is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 2035.010. Opposition filed by Specially Appearing Respondent Lincoln Electric Company. Joinder to Lincoln Electric Company's opposition filed by Specially Appearing Respondent Reliance Electric Company. Opposition filed by Respondent Regal Beloit America, Inc. Reply filed by Petitioner. Adverse parties were provided notice. The videotaped deposition of Anthony Adams will occur by Zoom or remote technology within thirty days. All parties are to file a Special Notice of Appearance within two Court days. If a hearing is requested, it will be on July 6, 2023 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely. Face coverings are optional for anyone appearing in person. Remote appearances must be arranged through Court Call by calling 1-888-88-COURT. No prior notice or permission is required. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code section 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must submit the proposed order to the Court after the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must submit it before or after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/SKF)
